title: Reading and Use of English - Part 4
description: For questions 25 – 30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and six words, including the word given. Here is an example (0).

questions.firstSentence: The company is having a professional translate the document.
questions.secondSentence: The company is (1) _________ by a professional.

questions.firstSentence: ‘Don't go too near the edge,’ the guide told the tourists.
questions.explanation: Tests reporting verbs with negative infinitives.
questions.secondSentence: The guide (1) _________ too near the edge.
solutions: warned the tourists not to go

questions.firstSentence: I'm sorry I didn't learn to play the piano when I was a child.
questions.explanation: Tests 'wish' + past perfect for past regrets.
questions.secondSentence: I (1) _________ the piano when I was a child.
solutions: wish I had learned to play

questions.firstSentence: Perhaps they didn't see the email until this morning.
questions.explanation: Tests modal perfect for past possibility.
questions.secondSentence: They (1) _________ the email until this morning.

questions.firstSentence: I only realized my mistake after I had sent the letter.
questions.secondSentence: Only after sending the letter (1) _________ my mistake.

questions.firstSentence: The exercise was so difficult that I couldn't finish it.
questions.secondSentence: It (1) _________ that I couldn't finish it.
